title: Anti-Terrorism Unit
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Organization |image= |name=Anti-Terrorism Unit |type=[[Law enforcement agency/Legends|Police]] [[Spy/Legends|counter-intelligence]]''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' |founder= |leader=*[[Jaller Obrim]] *[[Lon Shevu]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' |subgroup= |headquarters=*[[Coruscant Security Force Headquarters|CSF Headquarters]] *[[Galactic City Strategic Center|Strategic Center]] |locations=[[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |formed= |founded=Prior to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] |fragmented= |reorganized=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' |dissolved= |restored=Prior to [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] |affiliation=[[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]] }} {{Quote|They're competent at defense. They're not used to attacking.|GAR Clone Captain Ordo, discussing the Anti-Terrorism Unit|}} The '''Anti-Terrorism Unit''', also known as the '''Anti-Terrorist Unit''' or simply '''ATU''', was a specialized [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] division of the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]] designed to perform [[Spy/Legends|counter-intelligence]] work and combat [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] activities on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Though geared more toward defensive and investigative missions, as opposed to offensive planning, ATU [[Police officer/Legends|police officers]] were well trained in counter-intelligence and [[Special forces/Legends|tactical]] operations. The ATU itself was one of the most powerful units in the CSF in terms of jurisdiction and authority. Likewise, the ATU's commanding [[Captain/Legends|captain]] was one of the most influential [[Officer/Legends|officers]] in the CSF. The ATU responded to several terrorist threats notably during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the ATU was led by Captain [[Jaller Obrim]], formerly of the [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]]. Obrim and the ATU worked closely with [[Clone commando/Legends|Republic commandos]] to [[Mission to Coruscant (Clone Wars)|shut down]] a [[Separatist terrorist network|terrorist network]], and their close relationship continued until the end of the war. At that point, the ATU was absorbed into the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] and its functions transferred into other departments. The ATU was later reestablished by the CSF under the newly formed [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. Shortly before the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] between the Galactic Alliance and the [[Confederation]], several [[Coruscant terrorist attacks and riots|attacks and riots]] heightened tensions between the [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] and their fellow [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] citizens. While the ATU, under the leadership of Captain [[Lon Shevu]], had begun to identify potential suspects and terrorists, the GA government decided to form a secret police called the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] to better facilitate investigating and interning Corellians on Coruscant. Several cops from the ATU, including Shevu, joined the new GAG. However, most of the ATU cops left as the war continued and the GAG's methods became increasingly brutal and morally unjust. ==Description== {{Quote|There's law, and there's justice, and sometimes they're not the same thing.|Captain Lon Shevu|''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''}} The Anti-Terrorism Unit, abbreviated as the ATU and also referred to as the Anti-Terrorist Unit,''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' was a specialized [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] [[Spy/Legends|counter-intelligence]] division of the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]]. It worked closely with the [[Organized Crime Unit]] to develop intelligence on criminal and [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] organizations, with the intent of preventing attacks against the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The ATU had an expansive jurisdiction and vast legal authority, making it one of the most powerful CSF divisions. The [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in charge of the ATU was subsequently one of the CSF's most influential commanding [[Officer/Legends|officers]]. However, leading the ATU was also a heavy responsibility. It was not uncommon for the commanding captain to spend [[Standard day/Legends|days]] at the [[Coruscant Security Force Headquarters]] when a major investigation was underway.''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' The ATU gained more and more authority as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] intensified. Wiretaps and spying without reasonable evidence and of those who posed no threat became commonplace, and ATU [[Police officer/Legends|police officers]] could seize property without a warrant. Though ATU cops had training and experience in [[Special forces/Legends|tactical]] operations, the ATU was intentionally designed as a civilian organization, and the division itself did not have the setup of a paramilitary force. While that put the ATU on the defensive more so than on the offensive, it prevented the ATU from gaining a reputation as a brutal, secret police service. When some in the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] government sought to militarize the unit and give it the official duties of a secret police, the suggestions were quickly dismissed. ==History== ===Major attacks=== {{Quote|A major terrorist network lies in tatters this morning following the end of a massive overnight operation by Coruscant Security Forces. A total of ninety-seven suspects were detained or killed, and what's described as 'a significant amount' of explosives seized. Senator Ihu Niopua described it as a magnificent piece of police work and praised officers.|HoloNet News and Entertainment|}} [[File:OmegaSquad-TargetsPrelimart_SWI81.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ATU worked closely with Republic clone commandos.]] The Anti-Terrorism Unit was organized before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|start]] of the Clone Wars. Though [[Assassin/Legends|assassinations]] and bombings had occurred on Coruscant before, the Clone Wars brought a new and heightened danger to the streets of [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]]. The first, most disastrous terrorist attack was an [[Coruscant Administrative Sector terrorist attack|explosion]] in the [[Administrative Sector]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] which [[Death/Legends|killed]] several thousand citizens. The CSF responded by setting up checkpoints and closing down areas around the explosion to begin their investigation. The death toll and incident reconstruction completely overwhelmed the CSF and its anti-terrorism [[Detective/Legends|investigators]], who struggled to identify and catalog the remains.''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]'' Throughout the first year of the war, the Separatists initiated a brutal terrorist [[Separatist terrorist network|campaign]] on Coruscant with most of the targets belonging to the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] (GAR). By [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], the death toll resulting from the attacks numbered at approximately three thousand and forty beings. During that year, Captain [[Jaller Obrim]], a twenty-eight [[Standard year/Legends|year]] veteran with the [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]], was second to the CSF and placed in command of the Anti-Terrorism Unit. A few days before the anniversary of the start of the war, an [[Depot Bravo Five terrorist attack|explosion]] of a transport [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] killed fifteen [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], and the ATU began the task of investigating the crime scene in conjunction with the CSF [[Forensics Unit]]. To assist them, the ATU requested the help of the GAR's [[Special Operations Brigade/Legends|Special Operations Brigade]], specifically [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Kal Skirata]] and [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]] [[Clone Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ordo Skirata|Ordo]], along with [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bardan Jusik]] who was specialized in explosives. Together they investigated the area with the CSF's [[Scenes of Crimes Officer]]s. In the aftermath of the Depot Bravo Five attack, [[Director of Special Forces]] [[Arligan Zey]] unofficially and secretly authorized Skirata to conduct an [[Mission to Coruscant (Clone Wars)|operation]] to uncover the terrorists. Along with [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] from [[Omega Squad]] and [[Delta Squad/Legends|Delta Squad]], they began tracking the terrorist cells. The CSF's Organized Crime Unit came close to focusing an investigation on the [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] who got into the crossfire of [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underworld]] [[Thug/Legends|thugs]]. However, Obrim was able to deflect the investigation away from the commandos, allowing them to continue their mission. The commandos eventually located the terrorists, and in a joint venture with the CSF's ATU and OCU, they conducted raids that brought down the terrorist network. ===Increased authority=== {{Quote|I'll make it go away. I'm CSF. I can make all kinds of things go away when I need to.|Jaller Obrim, to Besany Wennen|}} On a [[Battle of Gaftikar|mission]] to the planet [[Gaftikar]], [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] commando [[Fi Skirata|RC-8015]], or "Fi," sustained a serious [[Brain/Legends|brain]] injury and was taken to Coruscant's [[Republic Central Medcenter/Legends|Republic Central Medcenter]]. Kal Skirata asked [[Republic Treasury Audit Division|Republic Treasury]] [[Agent/Legends|Agent]] [[Besany Wennen]] to keep an [[Eye/Legends|eye]] on Fi, and she discovered that the [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]] intended to euthanize Fi since they had declared him brain-dead. Wennen threatened the medical [[Droid/Legends|droids]] with her [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], forced them out of Fi's hospital room, and shot the lock to seal herself and Fi inside. In the resulting [[Standoff in the Republic Central Medcenter|standoff]], Skirata called Obrim for a rescue, and Obrim responded with several CSF patrol officers and half of the shift from the ATU. In a large display of weapons and [[Armor/Legends|armor]], Obrim and the CSF entered the [[Medcenter/Legends|medcenter]], stormed the hallway, and escorted Wennen and Fi out of the room. When they reached the lobby, they were stopped by a medical droid who insisted they had no right to remove the [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]] Fi. Threatening that he would release embarrassing information about the droid's [[Unidentified Republic Central Medcenter chief executive|boss]], Obrim was able to secure Fi's release. Along with Wennen, they left the medical center under heavy guard by the CSF. In the closing days of the Clone Wars in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], an [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|invasion]] of Coruscant by the Separatists left the Republic reeling, and Obrim spent several days at the CSF Headquarters coordinating the response efforts after the battle was over. When [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] was issued by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], declaring all [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] as enemies of the state, the ATU received instructions—along with the rest of the CSF—that their standing order to comply with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had been rescinded. Additionally, if they spotted any Jedi, they were to keep their distance and notify the [[Republic Military/Legends|military]]. However, due to the close relationship that many CSF and ATU officers had with Skirata and the Jedi who worked with him, several CSF officers, including Obrim, helped Jedi escape the ensuing [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|purge]]. Obrim assured Skirata that he could also ensure the loyalty of the ATU when Skirata began helping clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] desert the GAR. Soon after the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the ATU was absorbed into the new [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]]. Ironically, few in the CSF, including the former ATU members in the ISB, cared much for the new Empire. Later, Coruscant's law enforcement community would subsequently undergo a thorough investigation for anti-Imperial pockets.{{DB|character|chyler|Chyler, Tian}} From his new ISB post in an [[Coruscant Security Force Divisional Headquarters (ISB technology center)|old CSF building]], Obrim continued to provide support to Skirata as well as to the underground, anti-Imperial organization, [[Whiplash]]. ===A new civil war=== {{Quote|Are we doing riot control now?''"
"''That's CSF's problem. What we're looking for are faces we might know from the last job.|Ben Skywalker and a GAG officer formerly of CSF's Anti-Terrorism Unit|}} [[File:GAG_raid.jpg|thumb|left|270px|The Galactic Alliance Guard conducts a raid.]] After the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|end]] of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy-wide]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] and the formation of the Galactic Federation of Free Alliances, the CSF reestablished the Anti-Terrorism Unit. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], due to an increase in hostilities between the governments of the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and the Galactic Alliance, a series of terrorist [[Coruscant terrorist attacks and riots|attacks]] by [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] sympathizers and revenge attacks by [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] plagued the Galactic Alliance [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. The ATU, under CSF Captain [[Lon Shevu]], had initiated response efforts from the [[Galactic City Strategic Center]]. As the riots and attacks increased, the Galactic Alliance contemplated the internment of all Corellians and their sympathizers living on Coruscant. To begin the new security responses, [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Cal Omas]] offered command of the ATU to Jedi [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] along with a commission of [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]]. However, Solo felt it would would be better to have an agency separate from CSF conduct the raids and internment. That would allow the CSF to keep a relative image of neutrality and allow them to focus on general policing. Agreeing, the Alliance government formed the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]]. CSF officers from the ATU accompanied the [[967 Commando]] unit, which was transferred to the new GAG, on the first internment [[Raid on Jabi Town|raid]] in the [[Jabi Town]] section of Coruscant. Solo requested that Captain Shevu be transferred to the GAG, and several members of the ATU joined as well, all of whom were trusted and vouched for by Shevu. The tensions between the Galactic Alliance and Corellia quickly erupted into open [[Second Galactic Civil War|war]]. The former ATU officers brought their expertise in counterintelligence to the GAG, and several terrorist networks on Coruscant were quickly shut down. As the war continued, Solo became the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Caedus and, along with [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Cha Niathal]], staged a [[Solo-Niathal coup|coup]] ousting Cal Omas from [[Office of the Chief of State|office]]. The methods and tactics of the GAG became more brutal and less professional. Most of the former ATU officers left the GAG in disgust, and its ranks were filled with thug-like soldiers.''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' To undermine Caedus, Shevu remained with the GAG, though he missed his old job with the CSF and was eventually exposed and killed.''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' By the time the war ended and the GAG was dismantled, it resembled little of the ATU's model of a professional intelligence and police service that the GAG had striven to emulate. ==Notable members== ===Jaller Obrim=== {{Quote|Ask your chief executive about his interest in Twi'lek artistic pursuits on every fourth of the month, and if he'd like me to give police surveillance holovids of the visits to the cultural center to his lovely wife. Your call.|Captain Jaller Obrim, dealing with an obstructing administrative medical droid|}} [[File:Jaller_Obrim.png|thumb|right|150px|Jaller Obrim]] {{Main|Jaller Obrim}} Jaller Obrim was a Senate Guard who, during the Clone Wars, was transferred to and placed in command of the CSF's Anti-Terrorism Unit. While there, he formed a close relationship with Kal Skirata and several of the clone commandos. Together, they were able to dismantle a terror network on Coruscant and arrest its members. Although Obrim was a dedicated police officer, he was not above bending or breaking the rules if he felt that it was the right thing to do. Due to his high position in law enforcement, he was capable of helping Skirata's black ops mishaps disappear from official records. On one occasion, he used his authority to escort an injured Fi out of a hospital as a favor for Skirata. Obrim was one of the few non-clones trusted with Skirata's plans to help clone [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] desert from the Grand [[Army/Legends|Army]] of the Republic. Although he refused to hear specifics for reasons of deniability, he helped Skirata and his clan escape when Palpatine issued a warrant for the arrest of Skirata. Obrim was also well aware of the dangers of being a police officer on Coruscant, and he had a security system in his [[Jaller Obrim's residence|house]] to protect [[Telti Obrim|his family]] from the many enemies he had made during his career. ===Lon Shevu=== {{Quote|You trust Shevu?''"
"''Yes. There's a such thing as Force certainty, and I have it in that young man.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Cha Niathal and Jedi [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|}} {{Main|Lon Shevu}} Captain Lon Shevu was the commander of the Anti-Terrorism Unit before the Second Galactic Civil War and was in charge of the emergency response center for Galactic City until he was transferred to the GAG. He was a dedicated police officer who was known for not shying away from tough situations. He had strong principles on how to treat suspects and how a police force should operate. Shevu thus did not approve of Jacen Solo's methods, including Solo's treatment of his young, fourteen-year-old [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]] [[Ben Skywalker]] as an adult, and Solo's brutal interrogation methods. Eventually, that turned Shevu into a double agent; he used his high position in the GAG to spy on Solo for Cha Niathal and the Jedi. Shevu missed his job with the CSF and wanted to return to it,''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' but he was accidentally killed by the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Tahiri Veila]], who released [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] at him in an attempt to force Skywalker to reveal information. ==Behind the scenes== The Anti-Terrorism Unit first appeared in the novel ''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'', written by [[Karen Traviss]] and published on [[February 28]], [[2006]]. The ATU appeared continually throughout Traviss's [[Republic Commando|''Republic Commando'' series]], including the novels ''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'', published on [[October 30]], [[2007]]; ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'', published on [[September 16]], [[2008]]; and the series spin-off published on [[October 27]], [[2009]]: ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''. In ''501st'', it was revealed that the ATU had been made a part of the Imperial Security Bureau. The ATU also made a significant appearance in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'', written by Traviss and published on [[August 29]], 2006. The leadership of the ATU incites a pivotal decision in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series. During discussion, the characters choose to form the Galactic Alliance Guard in order to avoid using civilian police as a secret police. The formation of the Guard sets the stage for many of the events leading up to Jacen Solo's fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and takeover as the GA Chief of State. ==Appearances== *''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]'' {{Ab|The Clone Wars: Wild Space (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' {{1stID|Anti-Terrorist Unit}} *''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' *''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{CSFNav}} [[Category:Coruscant Security Force divisions and units]] [[Category:Galactic Alliance intelligence organizations]] [[Category:Galactic Republic intelligence organizations]]
